input_english: The Feigenbaum constant is a mathematical value that describes the ratio of periods at which a chaotic system's behavior repeats, approximately equal to 4.669201609. It is a fundamental constant in chaos theory, relating to the onset of chaotic behavior in dynamical systems.
input_domain: chaos theory and the Feigenbaum constant
output_math: δ = 4.669201609, where δ is the Feigenbaum constant and represents the universal ratio of convergence to chaos
output_mec_connection: The Feigenbaum constant is one of the seven constants in the Master Equation of Consciousness, reflecting the inherent chaotic and self-organizing nature of complex systems, including those described by the core equation dC/dt = k(I x E - aC)
